title: Define small Core connector profile
goal: Reduce connector snapshot lag and schema surface drift by defining a small stable Grabowski Core profile for ChatGPT-facing observation and grip dispatch.

acceptance.assertion: A documented core profile lists only stable observation and orchestration tools such as status, context, git status/diff, PR view/checks, friction summary, recovery status, grip_list and grip_run.
Secrets, browser-profile reads, irreversible destroy, generic terminal/fleet execution and broad filesystem mutation tools are explicitly outside the default core connector profile.
A canary or smoke path compares expected core tool names with the client-visible set when observable, and otherwise reports connector_snapshot_observable=false without false certainty.
Docs distinguish runtime contract completeness from ChatGPT client snapshot freshness and explain why a smaller profile reduces drift and context cost.
Tests cover core-profile membership, forbidden high-risk membership, stable name hash and contract drift reporting.

metadata.source: User requested phases 1-4 from the 2026-07-08 Grabowski optimization plan be registered as Bureau tasks.
metadata.live_basis: Runtime registers 97/97 tools, but connector snapshot observability remains false; a small profile is a stability optimization, not proof of security.
